title: gnaphalium microcephalum var. thermale
intro: Gnaphalium microcephalum var. thermale is a plant species, also known as the hot-headed cudweed, which belongs to the daisy family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and relieving inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds. The health benefits of Gnaphalium microcephalum var. thermale include reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and soothing digestive discomfort. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
